products.name: Chronos
products.description:
## Chronos


**Chronos** is a game of shard-collecting and timeline development. Players are **Time-Weavers** of the far future trying to secure temporal anomalies, historical landmarks, and ancient artifacts—all in order to acquire the most **stability points**. If you manage your timeline well enough, you might even receive a visit from an influential historical figure at some point, which of course will further increase your stability.


On your turn, you may:


* **(1) Collect Chrono-shards**, or

* **(2) Anchor a timeline card**, or

* **(3) Paradox-lock one card**.


If you collect shards, you take either three different kinds of shards or two shards of the same kind. If you anchor a timeline card, you pay its cost in shards and add it to your active timeline. To paradox-lock a card—in order to make sure you get to anchor it later, or, why not, your opponents don't rewrite it—you place it in front of you face down for later anchoring; this costs you a round, but you also get a **Tachyon Core** in the form of a wild-card token, which you can use as any shard.


All of the timeline cards you anchor increase your temporal influence as they give you a permanent shard bonus for later anchors; some of the cards also give you stability points. In order to win the game, you must reach **15 stability points** before your opponents do.
products.short_description: Chronos: The Paradox Engine is a game of thread-siphoning and timeline weaving. Players are Time-Weavers of a crumbling multiverse trying to buy historical nodes, alternate realities, temporal anchors—all in order to acquire the most chrono-points. If you weave enough stable threads, you might even trigger an event from an Anomaly at some point, which of course will further shake up the timeline and increase your chrono-points.
